acceptance_targets: [CH7-F004] PASS if Arthur's revised lines in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:519-528 include at least one practical offer/instruction and avoid generalized maxim phrasing; FAIL if he still delivers abstract thematic commentary detached from immediate task context.

[CH7-F005] PASS if the revised Peter exchanges in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:167-203 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:531-547 show at least two distinct coercive registers (not only bookkeeping/discipline vocabulary); FAIL if both exchanges still rely on near-identical ledger-style wording.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if Arthur's revised lines in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:519-528 include at least one practical offer/instruction and avoid generalized maxim phrasing; FAIL if he still delivers abstract thematic commentary detached from immediate task context.
findings.problem: Arthur's closing dialogue drifts into thematic thesis language ('Power cuts show you...') and weakens his established register as a reserved, task-first linesman who usually offers practical action rather than commentary.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework Arthur's lines in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:519-528 toward concrete, situational speech: what he observed, what he will check next, and what immediate practical caution applies. Keep insight implicit in action language rather than explicit aphorism.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised Peter exchanges in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:167-203 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:531-547 show at least two distinct coercive registers (not only bookkeeping/discipline vocabulary); FAIL if both exchanges still rely on near-identical ledger-style wording.
findings.problem: Mechanical reuse of Peter's control diction (accounts/discipline/schedule semantics) becomes over-literal and one-note, flattening dialogue texture and making his pressure tactics too predictable.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep Peter's accounting idiolect but vary control methods across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:167-203 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:531-547 by mixing financial language with social-status threat, domestic shame, or period-respectability framing so each confrontation lands differently.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on dialogue realism and voice separation: idiolect fidelity, leverage shifts, pressure cadence, anti-transcript behavior, natural colloquial texture (contractions/interruptions/slang when character-true), and pressure-true profanity usage per aesthetic risk policy.
revision_pass.label: dialogue/idiolect/cadence fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When edge cases arise, choose the more specific and potentially uncomfortable rendering if it is character-true and causally necessary. Intimacy, bodily risk, and moral compromise must be shown with concrete detail and aftermath, then integrated into later behavior rather than quarantined as a one-scene shock.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a draft abstracts pain, sex, coercion, blood, or economic shame at the exact moment consequence should be embodied, revise by replacing summary with physically and socially specific action tied to POV stakes.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High by default for spoken lines; uncontracted forms are marked choices for period formality, emphasis, or character-specific control behavior.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate structural engines across adjacent chapters: ensemble social scenes, inventory chapters, information-turn chapters, acted-upon pressure chapters, and aftermath chapters. Vary scene count (2 to 4), dialogue density, and interiority depth so consecutive chapters do not share the same pacing signature.
prose_style_profile.diction: Period-authentic but readable contemporary English, favoring concrete nouns and precise verbs over lyrical abstraction.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, alternating primarily between Elsie and Noreen, with occasional tightly limited deviations only when causal clarity requires it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Sentence rhythm should alternate compressed pressure bursts with longer observational lines that carry social texture; avoid monotone paragraph cadence.
